Sub_discipline_Interests: Molecular & Cellular Neuroscience, Synapses & Neural Circuits
Confirmed_Skills: PCR (Advanced), Western Blot (Intermediate), Confocal Microscopy (Advanced), Cell Culture — mammalian (Intermediate)
Research_Statement_FreeText: I'm fascinated by how neural circuits form and change during development, especially the synaptic mechanisms that go wrong in neurodevelopmental disorders. I've spent the last semester in a cell culture and confocal microscopy rotation, running PCR and Western blots on mouse cortical neuron samples. I'm hoping to join a basic-science neuroscience lab where I can keep building these wet-lab skills toward a PhD in neurobiology.

Sub_discipline_Interests: Genome Analysis & Variant Calling, Machine Learning in Biology
Hours_Available: Available less than 10 hours per week
Research_Statement_FreeText: I love turning messy genomic datasets into a clear biological story — most of my coursework has been in Python and R, and I recently used DESeq2 and BLAST to look for differentially expressed genes in a public RNA-seq dataset. I'm interested in genome analysis and machine learning approaches to biology, and I'd love to bring those skills to a computational genomics lab as I think about a career in biotech.

Sub_discipline_Interests: Translational & Clinical Cancer Sciences, Cancer Genetics
Experience_Level: Two or more years of lab experience
Research_Statement_FreeText: I want to understand how tumors evade the immune system, which is what pulled me toward translational cancer research after graduation. I've run flow cytometry, ELISA, and CRISPR-Cas9 knockouts in a cancer immunology lab for the past two years, and I'm now looking for a lab connected to clinical cancer research as I apply to MD/PhD programs.

Sub_discipline_Interests: Drug Target Structural Analysis, Protein-Protein Interaction Studies
Confirmed_Skills: HPLC (Advanced), AlphaFold2 (Advanced), PyMOL (Intermediate), Protein Purification (Advanced), NMR (Intermediate)
Experience_Level: Two or more years of lab experience
Research_Statement_FreeText: I'm interested in how a protein's 3D structure determines whether a drug can bind it, which is why I've spent the last two years learning HPLC-based protein purification and using AlphaFold2 and PyMOL to model candidate drug targets. I'd like to join a structural biology lab where I can connect computational structure prediction with hands-on protein work, on my way to a PhD in basic science.

Sub_discipline_Interests: Health Disparities Research, Global Health, Biostatistics
Confirmed_Skills: R (Intermediate), Statistical Analysis (Intermediate), Systematic Literature Review (Beginner), REDCap (Beginner)
Hours_Available: Available less than 10 hours per week
Research_Statement_FreeText: I care about health disparities and how research can actually change public health policy, not just describe a problem. I've done statistical analysis in R and helped manage a REDCap database for a systematic literature review during a summer internship, and I'm hoping to find a public health research lab connected to government or population health work.

I'm drawn to the intersection of computation and neuroscience — specifically how machine learning models can make sense of large-scale neural recordings instead of analyzing one neuron at a time. I've built Python and MATLAB pipelines to process EEG and calcium-imaging data for a computational neuroscience course project, and I'm looking for a lab where computational modeling and real neural data come together, whether that's a dry neuroscience lab or a computational biology lab with a neuro focus.

Confirmed_Skills: Python (Advanced), DESeq2 (Advanced), STAR (Advanced), GATK HaplotypeCaller (Intermediate), RNA-seq (Advanced)
Research_Statement_FreeText: I am interested in understanding how genetic variants contribute to complex disease. I have experience with RNA-seq data analysis in Python using DESeq2 and STAR. I want to pursue a PhD in computational genomics.
My research interest is in how the innate immune system detects and responds to viral pathogens. I have worked with primary human PBMCs and am proficient in flow cytometry and ELISA.
